newsSince: The catalyst on this name — Keppel Data Centre Fund III securing land for a 60MW greenfield AI data center in Ansan, South Korea (KDCF III ~73% SPV stake, construction permits and power approvals in hand, RFS by 2030) — was the June 9 announcement already incorporated into the prior 'up' verdict. No genuinely new on-thesis evidence since then.

sources.label: Keppel enters South Korea DC market, 60MW Ansan facility (DCD)
Keppel secures land for DC development in South Korea (Yahoo Finance)

verdicts.rankRationale: Under-owned-bottleneck master list: the Ansan DC pipeline (power approvals in hand) is exactly the physical-deployment exposure this page rewards, but it was the Jun 9 catalyst already credited in the prior 'up'. No new evidence since; hold the gain.
verdicts.bull: Ansan extends DC development pipeline into Korea with power secured; Seoul metro DC demand ~2.3GW by 2030.
verdicts.neutral: Catalyst now known and partly reflected; RFS not until 2030, long monetization lead.
verdicts.bear: Stock +5.7% since Jun 9 starts to re-rate the news; broad conglomerate dilutes pure DC exposure.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Execution and expansion of the Korea/Asia data-center development pipeline.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Long build timeline and capital intensity; conglomerate discount.
